tools.name: Pyomo
tools.description: Full-featured algebraic modeling language for optimization in Python. Supports LP, MIP, NLP, MINLP, stochastic, DAE. Developed by Sandia National Labs.
tools.docs_quality_note: Extensive but scattered. Can be overwhelming for beginners. Book available (Springer).
tools.quick_start:
pip install pyomo highspy

import pyomo.environ as pyo
m = pyo.ConcreteModel()
m.x = pyo.Var(within=pyo.NonNegativeReals)
m.obj = pyo.Objective(expr=m.x, sense=pyo.maximize)
m.con = pyo.Constraint(expr=m.x <= 10)
pyo.SolverFactory('highs').solve(m)
print(pyo.value(m.x))

tools.name: PuLP
tools.description: Simple, beginner-friendly LP/MIP modeler. Includes CBC as default solver. Limited to linear problems but very easy to learn.
tools.docs_quality_note: Good. Many tutorials available online. Clean API.
tools.quick_start:
pip install pulp

from pulp import *
prob = LpProblem('example', LpMaximize)
x = LpVariable('x', 0, 10)
prob += x  # objective
prob += x <= 10  # constraint
prob.solve()  # uses CBC
print(value(x))

tools.name: JuMP
tools.description: Algebraic modeling language for Julia. Excellent design, strong solver support, growing community. Preferred by researchers who use Julia.
tools.docs_quality_note: Excellent. Well-organized, comprehensive tutorials, clear API reference.
tools.quick_start:
# In Julia REPL:
# using Pkg; Pkg.add("JuMP"); Pkg.add("HiGHS")

using JuMP, HiGHS
model = Model(HiGHS.Optimizer)
@variable(model, 0 <= x <= 10)
@objective(model, Max, x)
optimize!(model)
println(value(x))

tools.name: GurobiPy
tools.description: Direct Python API for Gurobi. Maximum performance and feature access. Tied to Gurobi solver only.
tools.docs_quality_note: Best documentation in OR. Clear examples, comprehensive API reference, active community forum.
tools.quick_start:
pip install gurobipy

import gurobipy as gp
from gurobipy import GRB
m = gp.Model()
x = m.addVar(ub=10, name='x')
m.setObjective(x, GRB.MAXIMIZE)
m.optimize()
print(x.X)

tools.name: CVXPY
tools.description: Domain-specific language for convex optimization. Automatically verifies convexity. Preferred for SOCP, SDP, and portfolio optimization.
tools.docs_quality_note: Good tutorials, clear examples. Focused on convex optimization paradigm.
tools.quick_start:
pip install cvxpy

import cvxpy as cp
x = cp.Variable()
objective = cp.Maximize(x)
constraints = [x <= 10, x >= 0]
prob = cp.Problem(objective, constraints)
prob.solve()
print(x.value)

tools.name: OR-Tools (Python)
tools.description: Google's OR toolkit for Python. Best for constraint programming (CP-SAT), vehicle routing, and scheduling. Not recommended for pure MIP.
tools.docs_quality_note: Good for specific problems (routing, CP). Weaker on customization and advanced features.
tools.quick_start:
pip install ortools

from ortools.sat.python import cp_model
model = cp_model.CpModel()
x = model.new_int_var(0, 10, 'x')
model.maximize(x)
solver = cp_model.CpSolver()
solver.solve(model)
print(solver.value(x))

tools.name: scipy.optimize
tools.description: General-purpose optimization in SciPy. Uses HiGHS as default LP solver since SciPy 1.9. Good for small problems, curve fitting, and NLP.
tools.docs_quality_note: Excellent. Part of the SciPy ecosystem with comprehensive documentation.
tools.quick_start:
pip install scipy

from scipy.optimize import linprog
result = linprog(c=[-1], bounds=[(0, 10)])
print(-result.fun)  # negate because linprog minimizes
